verses.commentary: Sexual immorality so severe even pagans don't tolerate it: a man with his father's wife. The church faces sin worse than the surrounding culture.
Despite this sin, they are proud instead of mourning. Their arrogance blinds them to moral catastrophe requiring church discipline.
Though physically absent, Paul is present in spirit and has already judged this case in Jesus' name. Distance doesn't prevent spiritual engagement.
When assembled with Paul in spirit and the Lord's power present, they are to act. Corporate gathering with divine authority enables discipline.
Hand this man to Satan for destruction of the flesh so his spirit may be saved. Severe discipline aims at ultimate redemption.
Your boasting is not good. A little yeast leavens the whole batch. Tolerating sin spreads its contaminating influence throughout the church.
Get rid of old yeast to be a new unleavened batch, which you really are. Christ our Passover lamb has been sacrificed, demanding purity.
Keep the Festival not with malice and wickedness but with sincerity and truth. Christian life is ongoing Passover celebration requiring holiness.
Paul's previous letter instructed not associating with sexually immoral people. Prior correspondence addresses earlier issues.
He didn't mean immoral people of this world entirely, or you'd have to leave the world. Complete separation from unbelievers is impossible and not required.
Don't associate with anyone called brother who is immoral, greedy, idolater, slanderer, drunkard, or swindler. Don't even eat with such. Standards are higher inside the church.
What business is it to judge outsiders? Aren't you to judge insiders? The church judges members, not the world.
God judges outsiders. Expel the wicked person from among you. Church discipline maintains purity by removing unrepentant sin.
